rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use const declarations for numeric limits in AGREE contracts when bounds are specified.
Generate boolean range expressions using 'eq' for clarity and type safety in assumptions.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be syntactically valid and placed in appropriate AADL scopes.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Use explicit type declarations in AGREE expressions to avoid type mismatch errors.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes.
Boolean expressions in AGREE should use explicit equality or comparison operators.
Constants in AGREE should be declared with type annotations for clarity.
anti_patterns: Do not assume numeric bounds for ports unless explicitly stated in the requirement.
Do not reference AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without model exposure.
Do not generate assignments in component type contracts; use implementation scopes for bindings.
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers unless the model supports it.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing constraints
Implementation-level bindings and subcomponent port usage
Model fusion and scope preservation rules
